channel.title: Information Technology on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Information Technology on RaihnForge

item.title: ITAM Project Management Internship Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My Spring 2025 internship at Kelvin Education focused on three core learning objectives that guided my development as a systems-oriented project manager and visual communicator. Building on foundational experiences from ITAM 490A, I sought to translate theory into practice by enhancing tool proficiency, streamlining operational workflows, and elevating cross-team communication through visual storytelling.

Objective 1 — Become Proficient with Jira

I deepened my knowledge of Jira by managing sprint cycles, grooming backlogs, and setting up project-specific dashboards. These systems were implemented across both the KC3 product pipeline and the Upper Elementary microlesson content initiative. I structured Epics and Stories to reflect evolving production workflows, enabling greater task traceability and faster throughput. Dashboards became key tools in daily team syncs, reinforcing accountability and aligning output with strategic objectives.

item.title: Basics of Computer Hardware Components
item.description:
Having spent years using and modifying computers, I had developed an intrinsic understanding of two primary aspects of the machine: hardware and software. While the distinction was always evident, I had previously given little thought to the nuanced categories of system software and application software, perceiving them as generic terms. The nomenclature and taxonomy of these terms carry far more significance than I had understood.

System Software vs. Application Software

System software and application software are specific and mutually exclusive terms (Learn Computer Science, 2022). System software refers to programs that interact directly with computer hardware: operating systems, device drivers, and similar low-level tools. These often remain less visible to users because their interface and organizational focus is the machine itself.
